title: fraxinus excelsior subsp. rostrata
intro: Fraxinus excelsior subsp. rostrata, also known as narrow-leaved ash or mountain ash, is a subspecies of the European ash tree. It is a deciduous tree native to northern Europe and Asia, valued for its medicinal properties. The bark, leaves, and roots of the Fraxinus excelsior subsp. rostrata contain compounds that exhibit anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, making it useful in the treatment of various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of ailments and promote overall health and well-being.
